description:
Body of `POST /test/key` (issue #10).

`key` is a GDK key name (e.g. `"Escape"`). The MVP supports `"Escape"` only, which dismisses the topmost open `GtkPopover` via `popdown()` — the reliable "achieve the semantic effect through the safe API" shortcut also used by `tap` (`emit_clicked`) and `set-value` (`set_value`), since GTK4's safe APIs cannot synthesise a raw key event. Any other key name returns HTTP 422 `unsupported_key`. The endpoint exists so tests can close a modal / autohide popover that would otherwise leave `tap` blocked under its modal grab.

`key` is matched case-insensitively for the common aliases (`"Escape"` / `"Esc"`); the wire form is forward-compatible — future keys can be added without renegotiating the schema.
title: KeyRequest
